memory.summary: I have a free afternoon but my term project is barely started and due Thursday. I can choose to relax now or do a quick chore before tackling the major task.
I spent half an hour on a trivial chore for minimal progress, which feels like a waste of time given my term project deadline.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial chore yielded minimal progress, which feels like a poor use of time given my term project deadline is approaching.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial chore yielded minimal progress, which feels like a poor use of time given my term project deadline is approaching.
I wasted the morning on trivial tasks, but I now realize that focusing on my term project will significantly reduce future stress and crunch time.
I spent 30 minutes on a trivial chore with minimal progress, reinforcing the need to focus on my term project instead.
Spending another 30 minutes on trivial chores is reinforcing my fear of missing the term project deadline, yet I lack the energy to switch tasks immediately.
Spending 0.5h on trivial tasks yielded minimal progress while my stress remains high, confirming that I must prioritize the term project despite my current fatigue.
Spending time on trivial chores yields minimal progress while my stress remains high, confirming I must prioritize the term project despite my fatigue.
Spending time on trivial chores yields minimal progress while my stress remains high, confirming I must prioritize the term project despite my fatigue.
Working on trivial tasks yields minimal progress while my stress remains high, confirming I must prioritize the term project despite my fatigue.
Spending 0.5h on trivial chores only advanced progress by 0.02 while maintaining high stress, confirming I must switch to the term project immediately.

goals.description: Complete the trivial easy chore to clear it from the list immediately.
goals.rationale: The task is labeled 'instant win' with a deadline of Monday, making it quick to finish and low risk of missing if done now. Clearing small tasks reduces cognitive load.

goals.description: Work on the term project for at least 30 minutes to increase progress from 25%.
goals.rationale: The project is due in 3 days (Thursday) with only 25% completion. Continuing work now mitigates the risk of the deadline approaching before significant work is done.

agent.last_reasoning: This clears the high-priority 'instant win' due in 2 hours, reducing cognitive load before tackling the term project. I weighed this against my low energy (1.8) and stress, but completing small tasks now prevents deadline anxiety.
